meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between theory and production. Learn how to overcome no SaaS knowledge through portfolio building using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of SaaS knowledge, stop studying theory and start building production-grade micro-SaaS projects. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform abstract concepts into a tangible portfolio that proves your technical competency to future employers and clients.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, paralyzed by the sheer mountain of documentation between you and your first SaaS launch. The industry calls it 'no SaaS knowledge,' but in reality, it's the friction of shifting from student to engineer. You don't need another tutorial; you need a battlefield where your code actually runs.

Today, we strip away the abstraction. By treating your portfolio as a series of deployed, high-utility micro-apps rather than static homework, you bypass the gatekeepers. We are using Antigravity to define architecture and Claude Code to execute the build, turning your lack of experience into a documented track record of shipped software.

steps.title: Define the Micro-SaaS Scope
steps.description: Narrow your focus to a single, high-value utility that solves one specific problem. This prevents scope creep and ensures your first project is shippable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Define a MVP for a tool that solves [INSERT PROBLEM]. Output a 3-tier architecture plan, database schema, and a list of essential API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project roadmap based on the Antigravity architecture plan.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Generate your boilerplate to handle authentication and database connections immediately, avoiding the 'blank canvas' trap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js boilerplate with Supabase integration, Tailwind CSS, and Stripe checkout flow for the architecture defined in Step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project folder and apply the Antigravity scaffold structure.'

steps.title: Iterative Logic Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out your core business logic, ensuring each function is unit-tested as you go.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for the primary service: [INSERT SERVICE]. Ensure it includes edge-case handling for null values and API errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path /src/services/core-logic.ts --prompt 'Implement the logic generated by Antigravity with robust error handling.'

steps.title: Testing and Refinement
steps.description: Automate your testing suite to ensure that every feature added to your portfolio is production-ready and bug-free.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Vitest unit tests for the core service logic to ensure 90% coverage and document any potential race conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run tests and fix any failing assertions found in the new test suite.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Deploy your app to Vercel and write a 'Technical Post-Mortem' that explains your design decisions—this is what recruiters actually read.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that includes the architecture diagram, technical challenges solved, and instructions for local environment setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Finalize the project documentation and verify deployment configuration.'

pro_tips: Focus on the 'Why' in your README; recruiters care more about your decision-making process than your code syntax.
Use Claude Code to explain code you don't understand; treat it as an on-demand senior mentor during your build process.
Always implement logging from day one; debugging a live SaaS without observability is where most student projects die.
Treat your database schema as the source of truth for your entire application logic to maintain consistency.
Prioritize shipping a 'boring' working app over a 'complex' broken app; reliability is the most attractive trait for junior developers.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, iterate by adding a secondary feature based on user feedback or refactor for performance to show growth in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build what others only talk about. Don't let your code sit on a local machine—ship it, iterate, and master the craft. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
